item.title: About
item.description: I am an Industrial Engineer graduate and a Data Science Master’s student. Currently, my research interest is computer vision and natural language processing. I created this website to share my knowledge, document my course material and put some of my interesting codes. All the posts will be mainly about machine learning, statistics and python.

item.title: Big Data computing
item.description: —————— MAP REDUCE —————— Distributed File System (DFS) Modern data-mining applications, often called “big-data” analysis, require us to manage immense amounts of data quickly. The key factor of making fast computation is parallelization and this is done via computer clusters. To exploit cluster computing, files must look and behave somewhat differently from the conventional file systems found on single computers. This new file system is called Distributed File System (DFS).

item.title: Combinatorial optimization with Deep Learning
item.description: POINTER NETWORKS https://arxiv.org/pdf/1506.03134.pdf NEURAL COMBINATORIAL OPTIMIZATION WITH REINFORCEMENT LEARNING https://openreview.net/pdf?id=Bk9mxlSFx ATTENTION, LEARN TO SOLVE ROUTING PROBLEMS! https://arxiv.org/pdf/1803.08475v3.pdf

item.title: Deep Reinforcement Learning
item.description: This is a blog post about the UCL course on Reinforcement Learning by David Silver. The course is divided into 10 Lectures and I will share my notes on every lecture in this post. LECTURE 1(Introduction to RL) Characteristic of Reinforcement Learning There is no supervisor, only a reward signal. Feedback is delayed, not instantaneous. Time matters, (sequential, not i.i.d). Agent’s actions affects the subsequent data it receives. Rewards A reward Rt is a scaler feedback signal indicates how well the agent is doing at step t.
